info_gaps: exact time of headache onset and time-to-peak

resource.note: last monitored bed — likely needed for this patient

warnings: Only 1 monitored bed remaining — allocate to this patient
BP 178/102 — do NOT aggressively lower until SAH/stroke ruled out (per R-STROKE-01 threshold 220/120)
3 other 'now' tier patients in queue with only 1 MD available — charge nurse must prioritize

hypotheses.name: Subarachnoid hemorrhage (SAH)
supporting_signals: patient states this is different from her usual migraines

hypotheses.name: Migraine exacerbation
rules_against: patient explicitly reports this is different from her usual migraines
thunderclap quality and neck stiffness are red flags not typical of migraine
must not be assumed until SAH excluded by CT ± LP

hypotheses.name: Meningitis / CNS infection
rules_against: abrupt thunderclap onset more consistent with vascular etiology

hypotheses.name: Acute ischemic stroke / hypertensive emergency
rules_against: no focal deficit reported in nurse note

immediate.action: CT head non-contrast STAT; place in monitored bed with IV access and continuous monitoring
immediate.reason: Thunderclap headache with meningismus and vomiting — SAH must be excluded immediately.

constraints_checked.result: 178/102 — below 220/120, do not lower per R-STROKE-01

immediate.action: Bedside neuro exam (GCS, NIHSS, cranial nerves, fundoscopy) and finger-stick glucose now
immediate.reason: Establish baseline neuro status before imaging and detect focal deficit suggesting stroke.

immediate.action: Hold sumatriptan and any vasoconstrictors; antiemetic (e.g., ondansetron) and analgesia per MD
immediate.reason: Triptans contraindicated until SAH/stroke excluded; symptom control without masking exam.

monitor.action: Continuous cardiac monitoring + reassess vitals and neuro status q15 min until CT result
monitor.reason: Detect rebleed, hydrocephalus, or deterioration in mental status.

monitor.action: Send labs: CBC, BMP, coags/INR, type & screen; prepare for possible LP if CT negative
monitor.reason: Standard pre-procedure labs and to support LP for xanthochromia if imaging non-diagnostic.

escalate_or_redirect.action: Page neurology / neurosurgery on-call now (neuro ETA 25 min)
escalate_or_redirect.reason: If CT confirms SAH, neurosurgical decision-making and BP/aneurysm management needed urgently.

explanation.summary: 44F with abrupt 'worst-of-life' headache, vomiting, neck stiffness, photophobia, and hypertension — classic SAH presentation that the patient herself distinguishes from her prior migraines. Vitals show tachycardia and significant hypertension but no hypoxia or hypotension. Tier 'now' (ESI 2) with high-risk features warranting immediate CT head, monitored bed, and neuro consult. Conservative bias applied — migraine cannot be assumed.

citations.signal: thunderclap headache with neck stiffness, vomiting, photophobia
high-risk presentation with severe pain 10/10 and concerning vitals
HR 104 — borderline, in context of severe pain/possible SAH
BP 178/102 — below 220/120 threshold; severe headache mandates neuro workup

checklist.task: Place patient in monitored bed, IV x2, labs drawn
Document GCS, NIHSS, full neuro exam
Reassess vitals and neuro q15min until imaging back
If CT negative and suspicion remains, prepare for LP (xanthochromia)
Confirm last-known-well time and time-to-peak headache

why_this_tier: Thunderclap headache with meningismus is a textbook SAH trigger per R-SAH-01
Patient explicitly states this differs from prior migraines — anchoring bias risk
Severe pain 10/10 with HTN and tachycardia meets R-ESI-T2 high-risk criterion
would_downgrade_if: CT head negative AND LP without xanthochromia AND headache fully resolves with migraine-typical course
Patient confirms identical character to prior migraines on further questioning and exam fully reassuring
would_upgrade_if: Any focal neurologic deficit, declining GCS, seizure, or new hypotension
BP rises >220/120 or signs of herniation
